scenario: Vanta Marketplace hosts 4 tenants and 9000 transactions per day in Camunda 8.8. The team's job worker uses the Java client and must restrict the activations to a single tenant id at a time, so workers running for one tenant do not pick up jobs belonging to a different tenant id.
question: Which Camunda 8 client mechanism scopes a job worker activation to one tenant?

options.text: Configure tenantIds on the activate-jobs command (or @JobWorker attribute tenantIds); the broker returns jobs only for the configured tenant ids
Configure a tenant prefix on the job type (e.g., t1__credit-check); the broker isolates activations by parsing the prefix on every activation call
Configure the worker to authenticate with a per-tenant client id; the broker associates the worker session with the tenant from the OAuth token claim
Configure a tenant custom header on every service task in the BPMN; the broker matches the worker's tenant header on activation by string equality

a.text: Correct. Camunda 8 client activate-jobs commands accept tenantIds (and the Spring SDK exposes the attribute on @JobWorker); the broker filters jobs by tenant.

b.text: Incorrect. Tenant isolation is a first-class field on the activation command, not a string prefix on job type.

c.text: Incorrect. While Identity scopes access, the activation filter is the explicit tenantIds command attribute.

d.text: Incorrect. Custom headers are static per-element data on the BPMN; tenant filtering is on the activation request.

explanation: Camunda 8 multi-tenancy: the activate-jobs command (and SDK worker config) accepts tenantIds; the broker returns only jobs for those tenants.
